title: Workflow Input Parameter
description: Definition of a workflow input parameter. Only 'name', 'type', and 'props' are valid top-level properties. All other settings (required, displayName, description, etc.) belong inside 'props'.

name.description: Input parameter name (must be valid identifier)

type.description: Input parameter type (e.g., text, number, integer, boolean, date, datetime, options, object, array, or custom entity types)

in.description: Parameter location for PublicApi workflows: path, query, header, or body

format.description: Parameter format hint for OpenAPI documentation (e.g., 'uuid', 'date-time', 'email', 'int32', 'int64')

visible.description: Whether input is visible in UI

default.description: Default value for the input (alternative to defaultValue)

schema.description: Input validation schema. Can be a type assertion (e.g., {type: 'string'}), an object field list (multiline string), or an array field list with types/conditions.

oneOf.description: Multiline field list describing expected object/array shape

additionalProperties.description: DEPRECATED: Use 'visible' and 'mapping' directly on props instead. Legacy nested UI/mapping metadata.

visible.description: Whether input is visible in UI (deprecated, use props.visible)

mapping.description: Maps to entity property path (deprecated, use props.mapping)
